channel.title: CodeSprintPro Tech Blog
channel.description: Practical backend engineering, system design, Java, databases, AWS, messaging, and AI infrastructure guides.

item.title: B-Trees and B+ Trees in Java: The Engines of Modern Databases
item.description: Understand the architecture of B-Trees and B+ Trees. Learn why these self-balancing trees are the standard for file systems and database indexing, and how they differ from Binary Search Trees.

item.title: Bellman-Ford Algorithm in Java: Shortest Paths with Negative Weights
item.description: Master Bellman-Ford Algorithm in Java for finding shortest paths in weighted graphs, including those with negative edge weights. Learn its intuition, implementation, dry runs, and how to detect negative cycles.

item.title: Bellman-Ford Algorithm in Java: Shortest Paths with Negative Weights
item.description: Master the Bellman-Ford algorithm in Java. Learn how it handles negative edge weights and detects negative cycles in a weighted graph, and how it compares to Dijkstra.

item.title: Bloom Filters in Java: Probabilistic Data Structures
item.description: Master Bloom Filters in Java. Learn how this memory-efficient probabilistic data structure checks for set membership with zero false negatives and controllable false positives.

item.title: Boyer-Moore Voting Algorithm in Java: Finding the Majority Element
item.description: Learn the Boyer-Moore Voting Algorithm in Java. Discover how to find the majority element in an array in linear time and constant space, a common and elegant interview favorite.

item.title: Kruskal’s Algorithm in Java: Minimum Spanning Trees (MST)
item.description: Master Kruskal's Algorithm in Java for finding the Minimum Spanning Tree (MST) of a connected, undirected, weighted graph. Learn its intuition, implementation with Union-Find, dry runs, and complexity analysis.

item.title: Matrix Traversal in Java: Spiral, Diagonal, and Flood Fill
item.description: Master various matrix traversal techniques in Java, including spiral order, diagonal traversal, and flood fill. Learn their intuition, implementation, dry runs, and complexity analysis for solving common grid-based problems.

item.title: Prim’s Algorithm in Java: Minimum Spanning Trees (MST)
item.description: Master Prim's Algorithm in Java for finding the Minimum Spanning Tree (MST) of a connected, undirected, weighted graph. Learn its intuition, implementation with a PriorityQueue, dry runs, and complexity analysis.

item.title: BFS (Breadth First Search) in Java: Level-Order and Shortest Path
item.description: Master Breadth First Search (BFS) in Java for traversing trees and graphs. Learn its intuition, iterative implementation, dry runs, and complexity analysis for level-order traversal and shortest path problems.

item.title: Big-O Notation in Java: Time and Space Complexity for Interview Problem Solving
item.description: A practical Java-first guide to Big-O notation for interviews. Learn how to analyze loops, recursion, collections, amortized complexity, and common mistakes with clear code examples.

item.title: Binary Search Pattern in Java: Efficiently Searching Sorted Data
item.description: Master the Binary Search pattern in Java for efficiently searching sorted arrays. Learn its intuition, algorithm, dry runs, and variations like searching in rotated arrays and on answers.

item.title: DFS (Depth First Search) in Java: Tree and Graph Traversals
item.description: Master Depth First Search (DFS) in Java for traversing trees and graphs. Learn its intuition, iterative and recursive implementations, dry runs, and complexity analysis for various applications.

item.title: Dijkstra’s Algorithm in Java: Finding Shortest Paths in Weighted Graphs
item.description: Master Dijkstra's Algorithm in Java for finding the shortest paths from a single source to all other vertices in a weighted, non-negative edge graph. Learn its intuition, implementation with a PriorityQueue, dry runs, and complexity analysis.

item.title: Dutch National Flag Pattern in Java: Efficient Three-Way Partitioning
item.description: Master the Dutch National Flag (DNF) pattern in Java for efficient in-place three-way partitioning of arrays. Learn its intuition, algorithm, dry runs, and complexity analysis for optimal solutions.

item.title: Fast & Slow Pointers in Java: Detecting Cycles and Finding Middle Elements
item.description: Master the Fast & Slow Pointers pattern in Java for efficient cycle detection, finding middle elements, and other linked list problems. Learn its intuition, algorithm, dry runs, and complexity analysis.

item.title: In-place Reversal of a Linked List in Java: Efficient Memory Management
item.description: Master the in-place reversal of a linked list in Java. Learn the iterative and recursive approaches, their intuition, dry runs, and complexity analysis for optimal memory usage.

item.title: Kadane’s Algorithm in Java: Finding the Maximum Subarray Sum Efficiently
item.description: Master Kadane's Algorithm in Java to find the maximum sum of a contiguous subarray. Learn its intuition, dynamic programming approach, dry runs, and complexity analysis for optimal solutions.

item.title: Monotonic Queue Pattern in Java: Efficiently Solving Sliding Window Maximum
item.description: Master the Monotonic Queue pattern in Java for efficiently solving sliding window maximum/minimum problems. Learn its intuition, algorithm, dry runs, and complexity analysis.

item.title: Monotonic Stack Pattern in Java: Efficiently Finding Next Greater/Smaller Elements
item.description: Master the Monotonic Stack pattern in Java for efficiently solving problems like Next Greater Element, Next Smaller Element, and related array/list challenges. Learn its intuition, algorithm, dry runs, and complexity analysis.

item.title: Prefix Sum Pattern in Java: Efficiently Calculate Range Sums and Subarray Properties
item.description: Master the Prefix Sum pattern in Java to optimize solutions for problems involving range sums, subarray properties, and contiguous segments. Learn precomputation, dry runs, and complexity analysis.

item.title: Sliding Window Pattern in Java: Efficiently Solve Array and String Subarray Problems
item.description: Master the sliding window pattern in Java for array and string problems. Learn fixed and variable-size windows, dry runs, and complexity analysis for optimal solutions.

item.title: Sorting Algorithms in Java: QuickSort, MergeSort, and HeapSort Explained
item.description: Master fundamental sorting algorithms in Java: QuickSort, MergeSort, and HeapSort. Learn their intuition, implementation, complexity analysis, and when to use each for optimal performance.

item.title: System Design: Building a Distributed Tracing Platform
item.description: Design a production distributed tracing platform with trace ingestion, context propagation, sampling, span storage, trace query, retention, tenant isolation, and cost controls.

item.title: System Design: Building a Service Discovery Platform
item.description: Design a production service discovery platform with registration, health checks, heartbeats, client-side and server-side discovery, zone awareness, rollout safety, and failure isolation.

item.title: System Design: Building a Session Management Platform
item.description: Design a production session management platform with login sessions, refresh tokens, revocation, multi-device control, risk signals, expiry, and safe cache-backed validation.

item.title: System Design: Building a Workflow Orchestration Platform
item.description: Design a production workflow orchestration platform with durable workflow state, retries, compensation, timeouts, signals, timers, idempotent activities, and operational recovery.

item.title: Topological Sort in Java: Handling Dependencies with Kahn's Algorithm
item.description: Master Topological Sort in Java using Kahn's Algorithm for ordering tasks with dependencies. Learn its intuition, implementation, dry runs, and complexity analysis for various scheduling and dependency problems.

item.title: Two Pointers Pattern in Java: Solve Array and String Problems Efficiently
item.description: Learn the two pointers pattern in Java with clear intuition, reusable templates, dry runs, and interview-style examples for arrays, strings, and in-place problems.

item.title: Union Find (DSU) in Java: Connectivity and Cycle Detection
item.description: Master the Union Find (Disjoint Set Union) data structure in Java for efficiently managing disjoint sets, checking connectivity, and detecting cycles in undirected graphs. Learn its intuition, implementation, dry runs, and complexity analysis.

item.title: System Design: Building a Distributed Cache
item.description: Design a production distributed cache with sharding, replication, eviction, invalidation, hot-key protection, consistency trade-offs, write patterns, and failure handling.

item.title: System Design: Building a Distributed Configuration Platform
item.description: Design a production distributed configuration platform with versioned config, rollout safety, snapshots, watchers, audit logs, multi-environment isolation, and safe client-side caching.

item.title: System Design: Building a Fraud Detection Platform
item.description: Design a production fraud detection platform with real-time scoring, rules, ML models, feature stores, case management, feedback loops, and safe decision workflows for payments and account abuse.

item.title: System Design: Building an Inventory Reservation System
item.description: Design a production inventory reservation system that prevents oversells across carts, checkout, payments, cancellations, and expirations with idempotency, hold timeouts, and operational recovery.

item.title: System Design: Building a Payment Reconciliation Engine
item.description: Design a production payment reconciliation engine that matches gateway events, bank settlements, internal ledgers, refunds, chargebacks, and reversals with idempotency, tolerance rules, and operational workflows.

item.title: System Design: Building a Search Indexing Platform
item.description: Design a production search indexing platform with change capture, indexing pipelines, mapping strategy, backfills, reindexing, partial updates, consistency trade-offs, and operational recovery.

item.title: AI Infrastructure on AWS: SageMaker, EKS GPU Scheduling, and Cost-Efficient Inference
item.description: Production AI/ML infrastructure on AWS: SageMaker real-time vs async inference endpoints, EKS GPU scheduling with nvidia device plugin, EC2 GPU instance selection (p4, g5, inf2 Inferentia), Spot instances for training workloads, and the architecture decisions that keep GPU bills under control.

item.title: Kubernetes for AI Inference: GPUs, Autoscaling, Queues, and Cost Control
item.description: A practical guide to running AI inference on Kubernetes: GPU scheduling, node pools, taints and tolerations, model servers, queue-based autoscaling with KEDA, admission controls, observability, and cost guardrails.

item.title: LLM Evaluation at Scale: LLM-as-Judge, RAGAS, and Building Automated Eval Pipelines
item.description: How to evaluate LLM systems in production: LLM-as-judge patterns with bias mitigation, RAGAS metrics for RAG pipelines (faithfulness, context recall, answer relevancy), BERTScore vs ROUGE trade-offs, building regression test suites for prompts, and the statistical rigor needed to trust eval results.

item.title: LLM Inference Optimization: Quantization, KV Cache, and High-Throughput Serving
item.description: Production LLM serving: how quantization (GGUF, GPTQ, AWQ) cuts memory by 4x, KV cache memory math, continuous batching in vLLM, TensorRT-LLM for NVIDIA GPUs, and the throughput vs latency trade-offs that determine your serving architecture.

item.title: LLM Observability in Production: Traces, Evals, Cost, Latency, and Failure Modes
item.description: A production guide to LLM observability: OpenTelemetry traces, token and cost metrics, RAG retrieval spans, eval results, safety signals, prompt/version tracking, dashboards, alerts, and redaction patterns.

item.title: MCP for Backend Engineers: Tools, Agents, and Production Guardrails
item.description: A practical backend engineering guide to the Model Context Protocol: host-client-server architecture, tools vs resources vs prompts, JSON-RPC flows, authorization, audit logs, rate limits, idempotency, and production guardrails for AI agents.

item.title: PostgreSQL Locking Playbook: Deadlocks, Blocking Queries, and Timeouts
item.description: A practical PostgreSQL locking guide for production incidents: how to find blocking queries, understand lock modes, debug deadlocks, use lock_timeout and statement_timeout, avoid idle-in-transaction sessions, and design safer migrations.

item.title: Retrieval Systems for RAG: BM25, Dense Retrieval, Hybrid Search, and Reranking
item.description: How modern retrieval pipelines work: BM25 internals (TF-IDF, k1/b parameters), bi-encoder dense retrieval, why hybrid search beats both individually, Reciprocal Rank Fusion, cross-encoder reranking with ColBERT, HyDE query expansion, chunking strategies, and evaluating retrieval quality with NDCG and Recall@K.

item.title: Spring Boot Timeouts: The Production Guide for HTTP, DB, Redis, and Kafka
item.description: A practical Spring Boot timeout guide covering server shutdown, RestClient/WebClient, database pools, PostgreSQL statement timeouts, Redis, Kafka producers/consumers, retries, circuit breakers, and production defaults.

item.title: System Design: Building an API Gateway Platform
item.description: Design a production API gateway platform with routing, authentication, authorization, rate limiting, request shaping, canary releases, retries, timeouts, config rollout, observability, and failure isolation.

item.title: System Design: Building an Authorization Service
item.description: Design a production authorization service with RBAC, ABAC, policy evaluation, relationship-based permissions, caching, audit logs, consistency tradeoffs, and safe rollout patterns.

item.title: System Design: Building a Data Export Platform
item.description: Design a production data export platform for CSV, JSON, and Parquet exports with async jobs, snapshots, object storage, authorization, audit logs, retention, throttling, and failure recovery.

item.title: System Design: Building a Distributed Lock Service
item.description: Design a distributed lock service for production coordination: leases, fencing tokens, TTLs, renewal, Redis vs PostgreSQL vs etcd tradeoffs, failure modes, idempotency, and safe usage patterns.

item.title: System Design: Building an Email Delivery Platform
item.description: Design a production email delivery platform with queues, templates, provider failover, idempotency, suppression lists, bounce handling, unsubscribe flows, rate limits, and observability.

item.title: System Design: Building a Feature Flag Platform
item.description: Design a production feature flag platform: flag schemas, targeting rules, percentage rollouts, local SDK evaluation, streaming updates, audit logs, kill switches, experiments, consistency tradeoffs, and flag lifecycle management.
